purpose: Recommended MCP tool catalog for Claude/AI hosts mapped to current MMPM HTTP API.

compatibility.note: All tools map to additive or existing endpoints; no breaking API changes required.

tools.name: session_checkpoint
tools.reason: Primary automatic memory capture tool (S15-3). Stores new atoms, tombstones obsolete ones, trains the session Markov arc, and commits to disk in one call. Claude must call this at session end and at each meaningful mid-session learning.

tools.name: memory_session_bootstrap
tools.reason: Single-call bootstrap payload with goals/constraints/preferences, relevant memories, and proof/version metadata. Primary session-start tool.

tools.name: memory_access
tools.reason: Deterministic Markov recall with proof metadata for sequence continuation.

tools.name: memory_batch_access
tools.reason: Efficient multi-item read for agent branching and planning.

tools.name: memory_atoms_list
tools.reason: Memory browsing with type/prefix filtering and limit/offset paging.

tools.name: memory_atom_get
tools.reason: Single-item lookup with full Merkle proof metadata.

tools.name: memory_atoms_stale
tools.reason: Memory hygiene — lists atoms not accessed within maxAgeDays. Use to identify obsolete states and facts before tombstoning.

tools.name: memory_pending
tools.reason: Queue visibility for eventual-consistency-aware clients.

tools.name: memory_weights_get
tools.reason: Inspect outgoing Markov transition weights. Use to verify reinforcement quality (target dominanceRatio >= 0.70).

tools.name: memory_verify
tools.reason: Standalone Merkle proof verification — no auth required. Confirms an atom was genuinely committed and has not been tampered with.

tools.name: memory_audit_log
tools.reason: Query in-memory audit log of mutation events (add, tombstone, commit, import, export).

tools.name: memory_atoms_export
tools.reason: Export all atoms as NDJSON for backup or migration.

tools.name: memory_atoms_import
tools.reason: Import atoms from an NDJSON snapshot produced by memory_atoms_export.

tools.name: memory_atoms_add
tools.reason: Low-level atom ingestion. Prefer session_checkpoint for normal session saves.

tools.name: memory_atoms_delete
tools.reason: Tombstone a single atom. Prefer session_checkpoint for batch tombstoning at session end.

tools.name: memory_train
tools.reason: Low-level Markov sequence training. Prefer session_checkpoint which trains as part of end-of-session save.

tools.name: memory_commit
tools.reason: Explicit flush to disk. session_checkpoint always commits automatically.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.reason: Semantic retrieval by meaning over active atoms. Requires semantic tools flag.

tools.name: memory_context
tools.reason: One-call context block with configurable token budget. Requires semantic tools flag.

tools.name: memory_policy_get
tools.reason: Read transition-policy constraints.

tools.name: memory_policy_set
tools.reason: Runtime policy updates for controlled prediction behavior.

tools.name: memory_write_policy_get
tools.reason: Read write-policy tiers that gate memory ingestion.

tools.name: memory_write_policy_set
tools.reason: Update write-policy tiers (auto-write, review-required, never-store).

tools.name: memory_health
tools.reason: Operational health for retries/fallbacks.

tools.name: memory_ready
tools.reason: Startup/readiness gating for tool orchestration.

tools.name: memory_metrics
tools.reason: Prometheus telemetry for monitoring and adaptive control loops.

tools.name: memory_weekly_eval_status
tools.reason: Exposes whether weekly scientific evaluation is due based on persisted run state.

tools.name: memory_weekly_eval_run
tools.reason: Executes weekly scientific evaluation and updates persisted run metadata/artifacts.
